steps.title: Use angle angle similarity
steps.content: The triangles already have one corresponding angle pair: angles $L$ and $R$ both measure $58^\circ$. One more matching angle pair is enough to prove similarity.

steps.title: Test the angle information
steps.content:
If angle $M$ is $72^\circ$, then angle $N$ is
$$180-58-72=50$$
If angle $T$ is $50^\circ$, then angle $S$ is
$$180-58-50=72$$

steps.title: Match all corresponding angles
steps.content: The angle measures match by correspondence: $L$ with $R$, $M$ with $S$, and $N$ with $T$. The triangles are similar by angle-angle similarity. The answer is D.
